title: SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM - 211info
text: METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading clubs, gender groups, conflict resolution classes, case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 1700 NW Civic Drive Suite 200 Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Grades 5-12 who are at risk of academic failure and their families Hours: Varies Email (971) 506-2609 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 506-2609 Heather Mackris [email protected] Alternate (503) 232-0007 ext 334 Director of Community Schools Meghan Zook Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Centennial, Gresham-Barlow, and Reynolds School Districts Services: Dropout Programs Family Support Centers/Outreach Mentoring Programs Parent/Family Involvement in Education School Based Integrated Services Tutoring Services Youth Enrichment Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - READY, SET, GO! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUN COMMUNITY SCHOOLS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CAFE PROGRAMS TRANSPORTATION - PORTLAND ELECTRIC WAYS TO WORK If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE
